_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Bone Tumour Excision, Excision of Osteochondroma, Exostosis_Medco.pdf
Bone Tumour Excision, Excision of Osteochondroma, Exostosis_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Presence of Osteochondroma requiring surgical excision.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of Treatment
X-ray/MRI labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

claim.label: Invoice and bar code of implant
Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure?
X-ray/MRI labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Was the Detailed procedure / Operative Notes mentioned?
Was the histopathology examination report submitted?
Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) of affected Part submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Type of surgery for Osteochondroma not documented.
X-ray/MRI not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right) of affected part at pre-authorization.
Clinical photograph of affected part not submitted at pre-authorization.
Histopathology examination report of excised tissue not submitted with the claim.

clinical_key_pointers: Osteochondroma is the most common benign bone growth, occurs between ages 10 and 30.
Mostly it effects the long bones in the leg, the pelvis, or the shoulder blade.
